compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome has undergone a catastrophic collapse of vegetative matter. The registry-defined specimens (P1-P4) are no longer present as viable plants.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The Pothos specimen has completely withered/detached. Only the white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4_Silver_Guest: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; plant material absent.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears desiccated; presence of white particulate matter (likely eggshell fragments) remains consistent across the timeline.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no new debris, but significant loss of biological material.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-4: Initial state shows Pothos leaf and P3 anchor.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Progressive chlorosis and postural collapse of the Pothos leaf.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_current: Total abscission/disintegration of the final Pothos leaf. The biome is now effectively barren.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: Total mortality of all registered specimens. The environment is currently non-viable for the listed species.
visual_health_inference.evidence: Visual absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue in all pots.

anomalies.intervention: Eggshell fragments observed in soil; these are not part of the original registry and represent a non-botanical addition.
anomalies.missing_elements: All primary plant specimens have been lost.

vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a total systemic failure of the botanical collection. Starting from the earliest image, the Pothos (P3) exhibited signs of terminal decline, eventually leading to complete loss. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) show no evidence of the registered plants, suggesting either total decomposition or removal. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment or decoration that failed to prevent the collapse of the biome.
